# WebPeel — Web data for AI agents

> Fetch any URL as clean markdown. Auto-handles JS rendering, anti-bot, 55+ domain extractors. One call.

## Install & Quick Start

npm install -g webpeel
webpeel "https://example.com"             # Static pages — instant
webpeel "https://polymarket.com" --render  # JS/React/SPA sites — uses browser
webpeel "https://cloudflare-site.com" --stealth  # Anti-bot protected sites

## IMPORTANT: When to use --render

Most sites work without flags. Use --render (-r) for JavaScript-heavy sites:
- React, Vue, Angular, Next.js, Svelte apps
- SPAs: Polymarket, Airbnb, Booking.com, Zillow, Indeed, Expedia
- Any page where content loads AFTER page render
- If you get empty/sparse content, retry with --render

Many popular SPAs auto-detect and render automatically (no flag needed):
Twitter/X, Reddit, YouTube, Amazon, GitHub, Polymarket, and 50+ more.

## CLI Commands

webpeel <url>                    Fetch URL as clean markdown
webpeel <url> --render           Fetch with browser rendering (JS sites)
webpeel <url> --stealth          Anti-bot bypass (Cloudflare, Vercel)
webpeel <url> --json             Full JSON output with metadata
webpeel <url> --clean            AI-optimized (no URLs/noise)
webpeel <url> -q "question"      Ask a question about the page (no LLM needed)
webpeel <url> --scroll-extract   Scroll to load lazy/infinite content first
webpeel search "query"           Web search via DuckDuckGo
webpeel search "query" --site ebay   Site-specific search
webpeel screenshot <url>         Take a screenshot
webpeel screenshot <url> --full-page  Full-page screenshot
webpeel crawl <url>              Crawl site pages
webpeel map <url>                Discover site URLs
webpeel batch <file>             Batch fetch from file
webpeel mcp                      Start MCP server for AI tools

## API

POST https://api.webpeel.dev/v1/fetch
Authorization: Bearer wp_your_key_here
Content-Type: application/json

{"url": "https://example.com", "format": "markdown"}

# With browser rendering:
{"url": "https://polymarket.com", "format": "markdown", "render": true}

# With stealth mode:
{"url": "https://protected-site.com", "format": "markdown", "stealth": true}

## Domain Extractors (no browser needed — instant)

55+ sites return structured data automatically via their APIs:
Amazon, Reddit, GitHub, Twitter/X, YouTube, Wikipedia, HN, ArXiv,
StackOverflow, NPM, PyPI, Spotify, IMDb, ESPN, eBay, Zillow,
Polymarket, Kalshi, LinkedIn, Medium, Substack, and more.

Example: webpeel "https://github.com/webpeel/webpeel" returns structured
repo data (stars, description, languages) — no scraping needed.

## MCP Integration (for AI agents)

Add to your AI tool config (Claude, Cursor, Windsurf, etc.):
{
  "mcpServers": {
    "webpeel": {
      "command": "npx",
      "args": ["webpeel", "mcp"]
    }
  }
}

Provides tools: webpeel (smart router), webpeel_read, webpeel_search,
webpeel_screenshot, webpeel_crawl, webpeel_map, webpeel_extract, webpeel_monitor.

## Programmatic (Node.js)

import { peel } from 'webpeel';

// Static page
const result = await peel('https://example.com');
console.log(result.content);  // Clean markdown

// JS-rendered SPA
const spa = await peel('https://polymarket.com/@user', { render: true });

// With auto-scroll for infinite content
const full = await peel('https://imgur.com/gallery', {
  render: true,
  autoScroll: true,
});

## Troubleshooting

| Problem | Solution |
|---------|----------|
| Empty/sparse content | Add --render (site needs JavaScript) |
| Blocked by Cloudflare | Add --stealth |
| Only partial content | Add --scroll-extract (loads lazy content) |
| Need all data from SPA | Check if domain extractor exists (instant API data) |
| Rate limited (429) | Wait and retry, or use --stealth |
